Identity.text: I am the man I am called to be. I am their hero. If it will be, it's up to me.
I am becoming the leader I needed in the past, the leader my family needs today, and the leader the world will need tomorrow.
I am wise. I am in control. I am abundance. I am energy. I am wealthy. I am free.
I am the man that finds a way to make it happen.

Why.text: I am here to give my children the greatest opportunity to express their highest capabilities and to pursue their dreams.
My dreams must be big enough for everyone to fit inside.
I create the man that I admire in every way, and I give that man to the world.
I am here to inspire, activate, educate, and liberate myself and others to create great value honorably.
I want to fix the food, the finances, and the education. I want to create more warm households.
Be the man I needed back then. Be the man my family needs now. Become the man the world needs tomorrow.

Standards.text: I care about the goal more than I care about my feelings.
Commitment is eliminating all other alternatives.
Focus is the amount of stuff I say no to.
It's not the gap. It's the gain.
I treat every interaction as if I paid ten thousand dollars to meet with them.
As a leader, my weakness is their way out. I live with strength and intention and purpose.

Pain Fuel.text: I remember the night Alexis got hurt and I had no car to go to her. Never again.
I remember the mold. We couldn't move, we couldn't replace anything. Never again.
My mother is done paying for dinner. I pay for the rest of her life.
One of the most insidious forms of child neglect is a parent who doesn't chase their dreams. I chase mine.
I am not in a state of need. I am in a state of must.

Vision.text: A sunset view from my kitchen. Dance parties with my kids every night.
The freedom to book a flight tomorrow with nothing holding us back.
A home so big my grandchildren always vote to come to my place for the holidays.
Driving through the mountains with my family, the windows down, Michael Jackson on the speakers.
Watching my children become the ultimate expression of their highest capabilities.

Priming.text: What am I happy about in my life right now? What about it makes me happy? And how does that make me feel?
What am I excited about in my life right now? What about it makes me excited? And how does that make me feel?
What am I proud of in my life right now? What about it makes me proud? And how does that make me feel?
What am I grateful for in my life right now? What about it makes me grateful? And how does that make me feel?
What am I committed to in my life right now? And how does that make me feel?
Who do I love? Who loves me? And how does that make me feel?
